topic.what: Trapdoor constructions using hidden factor decomposition in algebraic structures, particularly polynomial rings over Fp
topic.how: Extended bilinear expansion from mixed-term-only (HIP/X-1) to full bilinear terms with universal decomposition property; proposed Facto-DSA signature and EDF encryption schemes
topic.why_matters: Generalises factorisation-based trapdoors beyond specific polynomial settings; richer structure claimed to increase Grobner basis attack complexity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum degree reached during Gronbner basis F4 computation before full reduction
characteristics.vs_baseline: Regular MQ systems typically show higher dsolv
characteristics.context: Measured with MAGMA F4 on small fields p=7,11; EDF consistently lowest, Facto-DSA highest

characteristics.what_it_measures: First degree at which nontrivial reduction appears below expected degree for non-structured MQ
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 & 2: EDF remarkably low ffd=2 indicates early algebraic simplification
characteristics.context: With and without field equations; EDF shows anomalous early simplification

characteristics.what_itasures: CPU time for Gronbner basis F4 computation
characteristics.evidence: Tables show Facto-DSA up to ~53 hours vs EDF ~1.5 hours at p=11, n=8
characteristics.context: Small primes p=7,11 only; scales with n and m

characteristics.vs_baseline: EDF moderate, Facto-DSA highest at large instances
characteristics.evidence: Table 1: Facto-DSA p=11,m=15,n=16 reached 25559 MB before interruption
characteristics.context: Memory scales with system size; one Facto-DSA run interrupted

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of public equations in generated system
characteristics.vs_baseline: Variable dimension m can be reduced in X-1/Facto-DSA
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.5: EDF produces 2n equations in 2n variables
characteristics.context: Standard MPKC shape P=TFS; new schemes use full bilinear expansion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Change in attack cost when including field equations xq-x vs not
characteristics.value: Strong for small p, weak/reversing for large p
characteristics.evidence: p=7: all improved; p=11: X-1 still improved but EDF/Facto-DSA became more expensive
characteristics.context: First observation of field equation effect reversal with growing p

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average length of extended partition for degree n-1 polynomials over Fp
characteristics.value: Formula: sum_{m=1}^{n-1} (1/m p^m sum_{r|m} phi(r) p^{r/m})
characteristics.evidence: Section 2.3.1: derivation of average Omega(f) for monic polynomials
characteristics.context: Used in subset-sum decomposition step for trapdoor recovery

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relative hardness ordering of new schemes under Gronbner attack
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.3: Tables consistently show Facto-DSA most expensive; EDF easiest
characteristics.context: Only tested for small primes; extrapolation uncertain
